task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application is open and the window title or workspace indicates that the 'EPFL_BellaLui_2020' design file has been loaded.

task_completion.description: Create the Switzerland simulation with specified conditions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A simulation named 'Switzerland' is visible in OpenRocket. The simulation edit window or launch conditions tab shows the altitude set to 400 m and temperature set to 15 °C.

task_completion.description: Create the New Mexico simulation with specified conditions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A simulation named 'New Mexico' is visible in OpenRocket. The simulation edit window or launch conditions tab shows the altitude set to 1400 m and temperature set to 35 °C.

task_completion.description: Export flight data for both simulations to CSV files
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenRocket export dialogs, a file manager, or a terminal window shows that 'switzerland.csv' and 'new_mexico.csv' have been saved to the /home/ga/Documents/exports/ directory.
The OpenRocket window title changes to 'environmental_analysis.ork', or a save dialog/file manager shows the file being saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/environmental_analysis.ork.

task_completion.description: Create a comparison report documenting apogees
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the contents of 'environmental_impact_report.txt'. The text must include the simulated apogee values for both Switzerland and New Mexico, and explicitly identify which environment resulted in a higher apogee.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to configure simulations and export data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with OpenRocket's graphical interface (menus, simulation tabs, export dialogs) rather than just modifying XML files via terminal.

integrity.description: The apogee values in the report are genuine results from the simulations
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee values written in the comparison report match the apogee values displayed in the OpenRocket simulations tab.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by creating empty files
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV files and the new .ork file are seen being generated through OpenRocket's export and save functionalities, not created via terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base rocket file is /home/ga/Documents/rockets/EPFL_BellaLui_2020.ork. The target rocket file must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/environmental_analysis.ork. The Switzerland simulation requires 400m altitude and 15 °C (288.15K) temperature. The New Mexico simulation requires 1400m altitude and 35 °C (308.15K) temperature. The exported CSVs must be at /home/ga/Documents/exports/switzerland.csv and /home/ga/Documents/exports/new_mexico.csv. The report must be at /home/ga/Documents/exports/environmental_impact_report.txt.
